references.notes: These verses refer to Mecca as a sacred precinct (ḥaram) with specific prohibitions (محارمها), a concept central to pre-Islamic Arabian religion.
The mention of Thabir, a mountain near Mecca, as a place of safety for animals, highlights its status as part of the sacred geography of the Meccan ḥaram.
The reference to Tubba' clothing 'its structure' (بنيتها, referring to the Kaaba) describes the pre-Islamic ritual of adorning the Kaaba with garments (كسوة الكعبة).
The phrase 'fulfilled his vows' (أوفى بالنذور) refers to the pre-Islamic practice of making and fulfilling votive offerings or pledges to deities or sacred sites.
The description of walking barefoot to the sacred site is a clear reference to a pre-Islamic pilgrimage ritual, signifying humility and reverence.
This verse alludes to the 'Year of the Elephant' (عام الفيل), a well-known pre-Islamic event concerning the divine protection of the Kaaba from Abraha's army.
